name: Embalse de Villameca from Pobladura de las Regueras – circular by Omaña river
name_original: Embalse de Villameca desde Pobladura de las Regueras – circular por río Omaña
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Igüeña are characterized by the mountainous terrain of El Bierzo Alto in León, Spain. The region features significant elevation changes, offering demanding climbs and rewarding descents through diverse landscapes. Cyclists encounter varied forests of pines, oaks, and hollies, alongside picturesque river valleys carved by the Boeza and Tremor rivers. Prominent peaks like Pico Nevadín provide considerable elevation gains for those seeking challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Igüeña The most popular road cycling route is Embalse de Villameca from Pobladura de las Regueras – circular by Omaña river, a 53.7 miles (86.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and follows the Omaña river. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Bembibre loop from Brañuelas, a moderate 32.5 miles (52.3 km) path. This route offers scenic views of Bembibre and involves a moderate amount of climbing. Local road cyclists also love the Igüeña Recreation Area – Folgoso de la Ribera loop from Pobladura de las Regueras, a 37.2 miles (59.8 km) trail leading through the Igüeña Recreation Area, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Road cycling around Igüeña is defined by mountainous terrain, varied forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Igüeña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Igüeña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Igüeña?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Igüeña, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Igüeña?
A: Road cycling in Igüeña is characterized by the mountainous terrain of El Bierzo Alto, featuring significant elevation changes. You'll encounter demanding climbs and rewarding descents through varied landscapes, including forests of pines, oaks, and hollies, as well as picturesque river valleys carved by the Boeza and Tremor rivers.

Q: Are there routes suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, Igüeña offers routes for various abilities. You can find easy, moderate, and difficult options. For instance, there are 2 easy routes, 7 moderate routes, and 8 difficult routes, ensuring something for every road cyclist.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see on a ride?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical elements. You might cycle past stunning waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Paseo de los Molinos&lt;/a&gt;. Prominent peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Nevadín Summit (2,077 m)&lt;/a&gt; offer considerable elevation gains and panoramic views. You could also encounter the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Campo de Martín Moro Toledano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046217341/embalse-de-villameca-from-pobladura-de-las-regueras-circular-by-omana-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Villameca from Pobladura de las Regueras – circular by Omaña river&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 53.7-mile (86.4 km) route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38224515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Bembibre – Boeza River loop from Brañuelas&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 84.3 km with over 1200 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Igüeña?
A: The mountainous terrain suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially on climbs, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes in Igüeña are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31453294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Bembibre loop from Brañuelas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.5-mile (52.3 km) path offering scenic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igüeña Recreation Area – Folgoso de la Ribera loop from Pobladura de las Regueras&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Igüeña?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging ascents, rewarding descents, and the diverse natural landscapes, including varied forests and picturesque river valleys. The sense of accomplishment from conquering peaks like Pico Nevadín is also a highlight.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the region's landscape is shaped by rivers like the Boeza and Tremor. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046217341/embalse-de-villameca-from-pobladura-de-las-regueras-circular-by-omana-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Villameca from Pobladura de las Regueras – circular by Omaña river&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, follows the Omaña river, offering scenic views along the water.

Q: Can I find routes that take me through different types of forests?
A: Absolutely. The routes in Igüeña wind through diverse forests featuring pines, oaks, hollies, and yews. This provides not only natural beauty but also welcome shade during your ride, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10504128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tremor de Arriba – Tremor de Arriba loop from Pobladura de las Regueras&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there information on parking or public transport for cyclists in Igüeña?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists aren't detailed in the route information, many routes start from towns like Pobladura de las Regueras or Brañuelas, where local parking options would typically be available. For public transport with bikes, it's advisable to check local bus or train services for their specific policies on bicycle carriage, as this can vary.
tips: Pobladura de las Regueras is a small town located in the Bierzo region on the Temblor river. A good starting point for any adventure. The town has parking for caravans and camping. The most notable monument is the Church of the Assumption.

Beautiful mining town in the province of León surrounded by very interesting trails and paths for those who love mountain biking. In the town we can visit the church of Tremor located next to the river or the hermitage of San Esteban
La Garandilla is a small town located at the confluence of the Omaña and Valdesamario rivers.

Next to the Villameca reservoir is Quintana del Castillo, the head of its municipality and which, even though it was small, always played a role of some importance on the path of those who headed towards Astorga from the north. Little remains of the castle in its name, but its church is quite beautiful.
Villameca is a small reservoir in the Duero basin. Located north of the province of León. The reservoir collects the waters of the Tuerto river, a tributary of the Órbigo.

waypoints.name: Church of the Assumption of Pobladura de las Regueras
